Title: Where files live on disk
Summary: Where Bleepforge keeps its JSON cache, what is canonical, and what you can edit by hand.
Body:
## The two roots

There are two roots that matter:

- The **Godot project root**, set via [Preferences](/preferences) or env. This holds `.tres` files. They are canonical. Anything that ships is what is here.
- The **Bleepforge data root**, by default `data/` inside the Bleepforge folder. This holds JSON.

## What is in data/

```
data/
  concept.json                 Bleepforge only; the Game concept doc
  preferences.json             Bleepforge only; user prefs and themes
  dialogs/<folder>/<id>.json   cache, rebuilt from .tres on boot
  balloons/<folder>/<id>.json  cache
  quests/<id>.json             cache
  items/<slug>.json            cache
  karma/<id>.json              cache
  npcs/<id>.json               cache
  factions/<faction>.json      cache
  codex/<category>/...         Bleepforge only; project notebook
  help/<category>/...          Bleepforge only; this help library
```

## What you can edit by hand

You can hand edit `concept.json`, `preferences.json`, anything under `codex/`, and anything under `help/`. They are authoritative and will not be overwritten.

You should not hand edit any of the seven game domain caches. They are rebuilt from `.tres` on every server start, and hand changes will be silently overwritten on the next reconcile. Drift is the worst case (cache says X, `.tres` says Y, integrity check screams), and the machinery exists to keep them aligned, not to merge edits.

> note: the seven caches are still committed to git as a redundant safety net so historical states are queryable from either side.
